samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. ADD SUB MULT LS RS LT LTE EQ dout_valid dout_parallel counter

Receive 8-bit data in serial form and store it in an output register in parallel form. The input data is stored in the output register in parallel form, with the most significant bit being stored first.

For example, if the input data is 01010101, the output data is 01010101.

When the input data is received, the valid output is set to 1. If the input data is not received, the valid output is set to 0.

For example, if the input data is 01010101, the valid output is 1.

if the input data is 0101010, the valid output is 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Clock. Reset. 1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0). 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division. 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division. 1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present. 1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged. 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits. 0 1 2

Initialize the divider module. If the operation is signed, extend the sign of the dividend and divisor. Otherwise, use them as is.

Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected.

Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. If the operation is signed, handle the sign.

Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Reset to the IDLE state when the reset signal is asserted.

 Initialize the divider module. Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Initialize the divider module. If the operation is signed, extend the sign of the dividend and divisor. Otherwise, use them as is.

Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected.

Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. If the operation is signed, handle the sign.

Enter the DONE state when the division is complete.

Reset to the IDLE state when the reset signal is asserted.

 Initialize the divider module. if (rst) begin state <= S_IDLE; count <= 0; result <= 0; remainder <= 0; end Enter the DIVIDE state when a valid operation request is detected. else if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin state <= S_DIVIDE; count <= 0; if (sign & dividend[7]) begin remainder <= -dividend; end else begin remainder <= dividend; end if (sign & divisor[7]) begin divisor_reg <= -divisor; end else begin divisor_reg <= divisor; end end Perform the division operation by shifting and subtracting. else if (state == S_DIVIDE) begin if (count == 8) begin state <= S_DONE; end else begin count <= count + 1; if (remainder >= 0) begin remainder <= (remainder << 1) | (SR[15] & 1); SR <= (SR << 1) | (remainder >= 0 ? 1 : 0); remainder <= remainder - divisor_reg; end else begin remainder <= (remainder << 1) | (SR[15] & 1); SR <= (SR << 1) | (remainder >= 0 ? 1 : 0); remainder <= remainder + divisor_reg; end end end Enter the DONE state when the division is complete. else if (state == S_DONE) begin state <= S_IDLE; count <= 0; result <= {remainder, SR[7:0]}; end

samples.xml_description:
Converts 8-bit data to 16-bit data. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Input signal indicating valid data. 8-bit input data. Output signal indicating valid data. 16-bit output data. Parameters:
Stores the first valid input data. Flag signal to indicate the first valid input data. 16-bit output data. Output signal indicating valid data.

On each positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal, the module performs the following operations:

1. If the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0), it clears the flag and valid_out signals.

2. If the reset signal is not active and the input data is valid (valid_in == 1) and the flag is set, it concatenates the first valid input data (data_lock) with the current input data (data_in) to produce the 16-bit output data (data_out) and clears the flag.

3. If the reset signal is not active and the input data is valid (valid_in == 1) and the flag is not set, it stores the current input data (data_in) in the data_lock register and sets the flag.

samples.xml_description:
<module id="pe">
    <description>Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="c" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the accumulated result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAC_PE">Multiply and accumulate in a PE.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Accumulator Register: The module includes a register, c, to store the accumulated result. On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the positive edge of the reset signal (rst), the module updates the register. If the reset signal (rst) is high, indicating a reset condition, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the register (c) is updated by adding the product of the input operands (a and b) to its current value. Multiplication and Accumulation: Inside the always block, the module performs the multiplication and accumulation operation. If the reset signal (rst) is high, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the module adds the product of the input operands (a and b) to the register (c).</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description: Design a traffic light controller with a pedestrian button and a 60 clock cycle period. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button input. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. idle s1_red s2_yellow s3_green State transition logic. case (state) idle: begin if (1) begin s1_red end end s1_red: begin if (cnt == 3) begin s3_green end end s2_yellow: begin if (cnt == 3) begin s1_red end end s3_green: begin if (cnt == 3) begin s2_yellow end end endcase Counting logic. if (!rst_n) begin cnt <= 10; end else if (pass_request && green) begin cnt <= 10; end else if (!green && p_green) begin cnt <= 60; end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin cnt <= 5; end else if (!red && p_red) begin cnt <= 10; end else begin cnt <= cnt - 1; end Output logic. if (!rst_n) begin {red, yellow, green} <= 3'b0; end else begin {red, yellow, green} <= {p_red, p_yellow, p_green}; end

samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable bit for multiplication. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable bit for multiplication. Product. MUL_WIDTH is the width of multiplier and multiplicand.

Reset the internal registers.

if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 1'b0; mul_a_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; mul_b_reg <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; end

Update the mul_en_out based on the mul_en_in.

//mul_en_out is the output enable bit, mul_en_in is the input enable bit always @(posedge clk) begin mul_en_out_reg[MUL_WIDTH-1:0] <= {MUL_WIDTH{mul_en_in}}; end

Update the mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg based on the mul_a and mul_b.

//mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg store the input multiplicand and multiplier always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end end

Generate the partial products based on the mul_b_reg.

//Generate partial products based on the multiplier bits wire [MUL_WIDTH-1:0] temp[0:MUL_WIDTH-1]; generate genvar i; for (i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin: gen_partial_products assign temp[i] = mul_a_reg && {MUL_WIDTH{mul_b_reg[i]}}; end endgenerate

Calculate the partial sum based on the generated partial products.

//Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products reg [2*MUL_WIDTH-1:0] sum[0:MUL_WIDTH-1]; generate genvar j; for (j = 0; j < MUL_WIDTH-1; j = j + 1) begin: add_partial_products always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin sum[j+1] <= sum[j] + temp[j]; end end end endgenerate

Calculate the final product based on the last partial sum.

//Calculate the final product based on the last partial sum always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin mul_out_reg <= sum[MUL_WIDTH-1] + temp[MUL_WIDTH-1]; end end

Assign the output based on the mul_en_out.

//Assign the output based on the mul_en_out always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_out) begin mul_out <= mul_out_reg; end else begin mul_out <= 0; end end

samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[2:0]">Counter for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid" width_description="[2:0]">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Data register for serial output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. 
    The module has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and a valid signal (valid_out) to indicate the availability of serial output. 
    The module has a serial data output (dout) and a clock signal (clk) and a reset signal (rst_n) for synchronous operations.
    When the counter (cnt) is 3, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.
    Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.
    </implementation>
</module>

    <description>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. <module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input operand A (multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input operand B (multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output for the product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter>shifts[3:0]</parameter> <!-- Example parameter description -->
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="shifts" width_description="[3:0]">Counter to track the number of shifts.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="product_temp" width_description="[15:0]">Temporary storage for the product.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
The module performs multiplication of A and B using the shift-and-add method.
1. **Initialization**: On each clock cycle, if the reset signal is low, the shifts counter is reset to 0. Otherwise, it is incremented by 1.
2. **Addition**: The module checks the current bit of the multiplier (B). If the bit is 1, the multiplicand (A) is added to the product at the position determined by the shifts counter.
3. **Shifting**: The multiplicand (A) is logically shifted left by one bit for the next addition, and the process continues until all bits of the multiplier (B) have been processed.
4. **Output**: The final product is stored in the product output port.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.gvd:
module fsm
(
    input IN,
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg MATCH
);

reg [2:0] state, next_state;

parameter S0 = 3'b000, S1 = 3'b001, S2 = 3'b010, S3 = 3'b011, S4 = 3'b100, S5 = 3'b101, S6 = 3'b110, S7 = 3'b111;

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        state <= S0;
        MATCH <= 1'b0;
    end
    else begin
        state <= next_state;
        case (state)
            S0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S3;
                end
            end
            S3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                    MATCH <= 1'b1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state <= S0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Implement a Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S6">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S7">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S8">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="match_reg" type="sequential_logic">Register to store the MATCH output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <always>
                <case>
                    <case_if condition="RST">Reset the state and MATCH registers.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="CLK">Update the state and MATCH registers based on the next state.</case_if>
                </case>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic>
            <always>
                <case>
                    <case_if condition="state == S0">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S1">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S2">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S3">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S4">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S5">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S6">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S7">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                    <case_if condition="state == S8">Set the next state based on the input.</case_if>
                </case>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Implement a frequency divider that divides the input clock frequency of 100MHz signal, and the outputs are 3 clock frequencies: 50MHz, 10MHz, 1MHz.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="CLK_in">CLK_in</parameter>
        <parameter id="RST">RST</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_50">CLK_50</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_10">CLK_10</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_1">CLK_1</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Reset logic for CLK_50.</description>
            <signal id="reset">Reset signal.</signal>
            <signal id="neg_CLK_in">Negative edge of CLK_in.</signal>
            <signal id="neg_RST">Negative edge of RST.</signal>
            <signal id="or_result">Result of OR operation between neg_CLK_in and neg_RST.</or_result>
            <signal id="CLK_50_reg">Register for CLK_50.</signal>
            <signal id="next_CLK_50">Next value for CLK_50.</next_CLK_50>
            <always>
                <trigger>posedge CLK_in or posedge RST</trigger>
                <action>
                    if (RST) begin
                        CLK_50_reg <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        CLK_50_reg <= next_CLK_50;
                    end
                </action>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Reset logic for CLK_10.</description>
            <signal id="cnt_10">Counter for CLK_10.</signal>
            <signal id="cnt_10_reg">Register for cnt_10.</signal>
            <signal id="next_cnt_10">Next value for cnt_10.</next_cnt_10>
            <signal id="CLK_10_reg">Register for CLK_10.</signal>
            <signal id="next_CLK_10">Next value for CLK_10.</next_CLK_10>
            <always>
                <trigger>posedge CLK_in or posedge RST</trigger>
                <action>
                    if (RST) begin
                        cnt_10_reg <= 0;
                        CLK_10_reg <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        cnt_10_reg <= next_cnt_10;
                        CLK_10_reg <= next_CLK_10;
                    end
                </action>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Reset logic for CLK_1.</description>
            <signal id="cnt_100">Counter for CLK_1.</signal>
            <signal id="cnt_100_reg">Register for cnt_100.</signal>
            <signal id="next_cnt_100">Next value for cnt_100.</next_cnt_100>
            <signal id="CLK_1_reg">Register for CLK_1.</signal>
            <signal id="next_CLK_1">Next value for CLK_1.</next_CLK_1>
            <always>
                <trigger>posedge CLK_in or posedge RST</trigger>
                <action>
                    if (RST) begin
                        cnt_100_reg <= 0;
                        CLK_1_reg <= 0;
                    end else begin
                        cnt_100_reg <= next_cnt_100;
                        CLK_1_reg <= next_CLK_1;
                    end
                </action>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Reset logic for CLK_50: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, set CLK_50 to 0. Otherwise, set CLK_50 to the inverted value of its current value.
Reset logic for CLK_10: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, set cnt_10 to 0 and CLK_10 to 0. If cnt_10 is equal to 4, set CLK_10 to the inverted value of its current value and reset cnt_10 to 0. Otherwise, increment cnt_10 by 1.
Reset logic for CLK_1: On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, set cnt_100 to 0 and CLK_1 to 0. If cnt_100 is equal to 49, set CLK_1 to the inverted value of its current value and reset cnt_100 to 0. Otherwise, increment cnt_100 by 1.</implementation>
</module>

        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>

        <parameter id="ADD">6'b100000</parameter>

        <parameter id="NOR">6'b100111</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLT">6'b101010</parameter>

        <parameter id="SRL">6'b000010</parameter>

    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise. Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result. If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>

    The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
    The rising and falling edges are detected on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
    If a rising edge is detected, the rise output signal is set to 1. 
    If a falling edge is detected, the down output signal is set to 1. 
    Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0. 
    These output signals are synchronized with the clock and remain set to 1 until the corresponding edge appears again.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplicand.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplier.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="size">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <task>
        <description>Extend input signals.</description>
        <parameter name="length">size</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_a">mul_a</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_b">mul_b</parameter>
        <parameter name="output">ext_a or ext_b</parameter>
        <parameter name="operation">assign</parameter>
        <parameter name="value">input_a or input_b</parameter>
        <parameter name="extension">size{1'b0}</parameter>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Generate partial products.</description>
        <parameter name="i">loop variable</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_a">ext_a</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_b">ext_b</parameter>
        <parameter name="operation">assign</parameter>
        <parameter name="value">i?input_a<<i:'d0</parameter>
        <parameter name="output">pp[i]</parameter>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Generate pp_mux.</description>
        <parameter name="i">loop variable</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_a">pp[i-1]</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_b">pp[i]</parameter>
        <parameter name="operation">assign</parameter>
        <parameter name="value">i?input_a+input_b:'d0</parameter>
        <parameter name="output">pp_mux[i]</parameter>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Generate pp_reg.</description>
        <parameter name="i">loop variable</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_a">pp_mux[i-1]</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_b">pp_mux[i]</parameter>
        <parameter name="operation">assign</parameter>
        <parameter name="value">i?input_a+input_b:'d0</parameter>
        <parameter name="output">pp_reg[i]</parameter>
    </task>
    <task>
        <description>Calculate the final product.</description>
        <parameter name="i">loop variable</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_a">pp_reg[i-1]</parameter>
        <parameter name="input_b">pp_reg[i]</parameter>
        <parameter name="operation">assign</parameter>
        <parameter name="value">i?input_a+input_b:'d0</parameter>
        <parameter name="output">mul_out</parameter>
    </task>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description: Module name: calendar Input ports: CLK: Clock input RST: Active high reset signal Output ports: Hours: 6-bit output representing the current hours Mins: 6-bit output representing the current minutes Secs: 6-bit output representing the current seconds Implementation: The calendar module uses three always blocks to update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal. The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge CLK) or the positive edge of the reset signal (posedge RST). It checks if the reset signal is active (RST) and sets the seconds value (Secs) to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it wraps around and sets the seconds value to 0. Otherwise, it increments the seconds value by 1. The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the minutes value (Mins). If the reset signal is active, it sets the minutes value to 0. If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it increments the minutes value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged. The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the hours value (Hours). If the reset signal is active, it sets the hours value to 0. If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, it increments the hours value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged. Clock input Active high reset signal 6-bit output representing the current hours 6-bit output representing the current minutes 6-bit output representing the current seconds Hours Mins Secs Hours Mins Secs Hours Update Hours. if (RST) begin Hours = 6'b000000; end else if (Hours == 6'b101111 && Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011) begin Hours = 6'b000000; end else if (Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011) begin Hours = Hours + 1; end else begin Hours = Hours; end Update Mins. if (RST) begin Mins = 6'b000000; end else if (Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011) begin Mins = 6'b000000; end else if (Secs == 6'b111011) begin Mins = Mins + 1; end else begin Mins = Mins; end Update Secs. if (RST) begin Secs = 6'b000000; end else if (Secs == 6'b111011) begin Secs = 6'b000000; end else begin Secs = Secs + 1; end

<module id="adder_32bit">
    <description>Implement a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="S" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="C32">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="ADD=6'b100000">Add operation.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The carry-lookahead adder is a combinational logic circuit that computes the sum of two N-bit binary numbers (A and B) in parallel. The adder has two outputs: S, the sum of A and B, and C32, the carry-out bit. The adder uses carry-lookahead logic to compute carry signals for each bit position efficiently, allowing for faster addition compared to a ripple-carry adder. The carry-lookahead logic is implemented using combinational logic gates, such as AND, OR, and XOR, based on the generate (G) and propagate (P) signals for each bit position. The final sum output S is computed by adding A, B, and the carry-in bit (cin), while the carry-out C32 is obtained from the most significant bit's carry calculation.
    </implementation>
</module>

module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);
    
    reg state;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            
            state <= 1'b0;
            wave <= 5'd0;
        end else begin
            
            case (state)
                1'b0: begin
                    
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= 1'b1;
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                    end
                end
                1'b1: begin
                    
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= 1'b0;
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end
endmodule

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator module that generates a waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave". The waveform cycles between 0 and 31, which is incremented or decremented by 1.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State register used to control the state of the waveform generation.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module includes a register, state, used to control the state of the waveform generation.
        The module includes a register, wave, with a width of 5 bits, which represents the generated waveform.
        The state and wave registers are updated in the always block, triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
        On reset, indicated by ~rst_n, the state register is cleared to 0, and the wave register is cleared to 0.
        The waveform generation is controlled using a case statement based on the current state:
        If the state is 0, the waveform (wave) is incremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 31 (wave == 31), the state is transitioned to 1.
        If the state is 1, the waveform is decremented by 1.
        If the waveform reaches 0 (wave == 0), the state is transitioned back to 0.
        The waveform generation continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Design a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="s_idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1">State 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2">State 2.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3">State 3.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">State register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <always>
            <sequential>
                <clocked id="clk" type="posedge">State update.</clocked>
                <reset id="rst_n" type="negedge">Reset condition.</reset>
                <if>
                    <condition id="state == s_idle">Idle state condition.</condition>
                    <then>
                        <sequential>
                            <if>
                                <condition id="data_in == 0">Data input is 0.</condition>
                                <then>
                                    <assign id="state = s1">Transition to state 1.</assign>
                                </then>
                                <else>
                                    <assign id="state = s_idle">Stay in idle state.</assign>
                                </else>
                            </if>
                        </sequential>
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <sequential>
                            <if>
                                <condition id="state == s1">State 1 condition.</condition>
                                <then>
                                    <sequential>
                                        <if>
                                            <condition id="data_in == 1">Data input is 1.</condition>
                                            <then>
                                                <assign id="state = s2">Transition to state 2.</assign>
                                            </then>
                                            <else>
                                                <assign id="state = s1">Stay in state 1.</assign>
                                            </else>
                                        </if>
                                    </sequential>
                                </then>
                                <else>
                                    <sequential>
                                        <if>
                                            <condition id="data_in == 0">Data input is 0.</condition>
                                            <then>
                                                <assign id="state = s1">Transition to state 1.</assign>
                                            </then>
                                            <else>
                                                <assign id="state = s2">Transition to state 2.</assign>
                                            </else>
                                        </if>
                                    </sequential>
                                </else>
                            </if>
                        </sequential>
                    </else>
                </if>
            </sequential>
        </always>
        <always>
            <combinational>
                <if>
                    <condition id="state == s2">State 2 condition.</condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign id="data_out = 1">Set data output to 1.</assign>
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <assign id="data_out = 0">Set data output to 0.</assign>
                    </else>
                </if>
            </combinational>
        </always>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate input data in 4 cycles and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="INPUT">1</parameter>
        <parameter name="ACCUMULATE">2</parameter>
        <parameter name="OUTPUT">3</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State register for the accumulator.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count" width_description="[1:0]">Counter register for input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sum" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulated sum register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module operates in a state machine fashion with four states: IDLE, INPUT, ACCUMULATE, and OUTPUT.
        - In the IDLE state, the state waits for valid_in to be high to transition to the INPUT state.
        - In the INPUT state, it accumulates input data_in values until the count reaches 3 (2'b11).
        - In the ACCUMULATE state, it adds the accumulated sum to the current data_in.
        - In the OUTPUT state, it sets valid_out high and transitions back to the IDLE state.
    </implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n
);
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter (torsional ring counter).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">Current count value.</output>

    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">64</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The counter has N bits, and the next state is either the current state shifted right by one with a 1 appended to the most significant bit if the least significant bit is 0, or the current state shifted right by one with a 0 appended to the most significant bit if the least significant bit is 1.

</module>

<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">6</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">8</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="i" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">Loop variable for the for loop.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    <task>
        <description>Initialize the RAM array to all zeros.</description>
        <implementation>for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin RAM[i] = 0; end</implementation>
    </task>
    <description>When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <always_comb>
        <if>
            <condition>~rst_n</condition>
            <true>
                <description>Reset the read_data to all zeros.</description>
                <assignment>read_data = 6'b000000;</assignment>
            </true>
            <false>
                <if>
                    <condition>read_en</condition>
                    <true>
                        <description>Read data from the RAM array.</description>
                        <assignment>read_data = RAM[read_addr];</assignment>
                    </true>
                    <false>
                        <description>Write data to the RAM array.</description>
                        <assignment>RAM[write_addr] = write_data;</assignment>
                    </false>
                </if>
            </false>
        </if>
    </always_comb>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Stores input data signal. Stores input enable signal. Controls selection of input data. Controls selection of input data. Stores input data signal. Stores input enable signal. Controls selection of input data. Controls selection of input data.

Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer.

Register data_reg to store the input data signal.

Register en_data_reg to store the input enable signal.

Register en_clap_one and en_clap_two to control the selection of input data.

Assign dataout based on the value of en_clap_two.
